crossref.abstract: <jats:p>Kagome metals offer a unique platform for investigating robust electron-correlation effects because of their lattice geometry, flat bands, and multiorbital nature. In the cases with active flat bands, recent theoretical studies have pointed to a rich phase diagram that contains not only electronic orders but also quantum criticality. Very recently, <a:math xmlns:a="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><a:mrow><a:msub><a:mi>CsCr</a:mi><a:mn>3</a:mn></a:msub><a:msub><a:mi>Sb</a:mi><a:mn>5</a:mn></a:msub></a:mrow></a:math> has emerged as a strong candidate for exploring such new physics. Here, using effective tight-binding models obtained from  calculations, we study the effects of electronic correlations and symmetries on the electronic structure of <b:math xmlns:b="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><b:mrow><b:msub><b:mi>CsCr</b:mi><b:mn>3</b:mn></b:msub><b:msub><b:mi>Sb</b:mi><b:mn>5</b:mn></b:msub></b:mrow></b:math>. The effective tight-binding model and Fermi surface comprise multiple <c:math xmlns:c="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><c:mrow><c:mi>Cr</c:mi><c:mtext>−</c:mtext><c:mi>d</c:mi></c:mrow></c:math> orbitals and <d:math xmlns:d="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><d:mrow><d:mi>Sb</d:mi><d:mtext>−</d:mtext><d:mi>p</d:mi></d:mrow></d:math> orbitals. The introduction of Hubbard-Kanamori interactions leads to orbital-selective band renormalization dominated by the <e:math xmlns:e="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><e:msub><e:mi>d</e:mi><e:mrow><e:mi>x</e:mi><e:mi>z</e:mi></e:mrow></e:msub></e:math> band, concurrently producing emergent flat bands very close to the Fermi level. Our analysis sets the stage for further investigations into the electronic properties of <f:math xmlns:f="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><f:mrow><f:msub><f:mi>CsCr</f:mi><f:mn>3</f:mn></f:msub><f:msub><f:mi>Sb</f:mi><f:mn>5</f:mn></f:msub></f:mrow></f:math>, including electronic orders, quantum criticality, and unconventional superconductivity, which promise to shed much new light into the electronic materials with frustrated lattices and bring about new connections with the correlation physics of a variety of strongly correlated systems.</jats:p>
title:
Electron correlations in the kagome flat band metal 
<mml:math xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mml:mrow><mml:msub><mml:mi>CsCr</mml:mi><mml:mn>3</mml:mn></mml:msub><mml:msub><mml:mi>Sb</mml:mi><mml:mn>5</mml:mn></mml:msub></mml:mrow></mml:math>
